entry.title: How to Use SAP Extensibility Wizard in SAP S/4HANA Clean Core
entry.content:
A Comprehensive Guide to Extending SAP S/4HANA with the SAP Extensibility Wizard — Without Compromising Your Clean Core

Introduction

Extensibility is a critical aspect of SAP S/4HANA, enabling organizations to tailor the system to their unique business needs while maintaining stability and upgrade readiness. In this guide, we will explore the SAP Extensibility Wizard, a powerful tool designed to help developers extend SAP S/4HANA in a clean and controlled way.

What Is the SAP Extensibility Wizard?

The SAP Extensibility Wizard is a guided tool provided by SAP that simplifies the process of creating extensions. It ensures that developers can add custom functionality without modifying the core system, thereby preserving the clean core principle. This approach guarantees that upgrades remain smooth and that the system stays resilient over time.

Availability: Public Cloud vs. Private Cloud

A common question arises: Is the SAP Extensibility Wizard available only in the Public Cloud, or also in the Private Cloud?

  • Public Cloud (SAP S/4HANA Cloud – Public Edition):
    The wizard is primarily available here. It supports clean-core extensibility and helps organizations build safe, upgrade‑friendly extensions.
  • Private Cloud / On‑Premise:
    Extensibility is possible through other approaches such as in‑app extensibility, ABAP Cloud, and side‑by‑side extensions on SAP BTP. However, the wizard itself is not provided in the same way as in the Public Cloud.

In summary:

  • Public Cloud → Wizard available.
  • Private Cloud / On‑Premise → Extensibility options exist, but without the wizard.

Focus of This Blog

This blog will focus on the SAP Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud – Public Edition.

How to Use the Extensibility Wizard

With the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition extensibility wizard, developers can:

  • Create on‑stack extensions such as custom fields and custom logic.
  • Build side‑by‑side extensions like custom processes using SAP Build Process Automation.

This guided approach ensures that extensions are implemented efficiently, securely, and in alignment with SAP’s clean‑core strategy.

Conclusion

The SAP Extensibility Wizard is a key enabler for organizations running SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. By leveraging this tool, businesses can extend their systems with confidence, ensuring that customizations remain upgrade‑safe and future‑proof.

 

Detailed Steps:

Use Case : We will create a sales order approval process .

1. Navigate to SAP S4/HANA Fiori Launchpad.

1.1 Select Business Application Manage Sales Orders. 

1.2  Navigate to Create Extension

 

1.3 In this case , we will select custom process.

The process can start based on various triggers 

1.4 Select Business Event from SAP S4/HANA, that Triggers the process

1.5 Select Event Object

1.6 Select the SalesOrder create event.

1.7 Click Preview to see all the options you selected on previous screens, and make any necessary corrections.

1.8 Click Create , to create the process in SAP Build.

1.9 The extension is now available and can also be opened in SAP Build.

1.10 When you select Open Extension, the system launches another tab that opens the process.

1.11 The Process Automation project overview page is now displayed. Select SalesOrder Approval.

1.12 Extension is now openEd in SAP Build , Start design of the process.

The Business context is carried over from SAP S4/HANA to SAP Build .

1.13 You can add an action, form , or approval . To get further details on the sales order, you need to call an action .

1.14 In this use case , we will call sales order read API , to fetch the sales order details.

1.15 Browse the library to search for  the relevant APIs .

1.16 Select read sales order API to get details of the sales order, The business context and system ID were automatically pulled in from SAP S4/HANA .

1.17 The action to read the sales order has now been added . Now users can design the process according to their needs. The user now wants to initiate a workflow that sends an email to the approver for sales order approval .

1.18 The email will include the sales order details , allowing approver to review and approve it . we're utilizing AI to generate the process , Enter the prompt to add an approval step and send the sales order details to the approver via email.

1.19 AI has now added the process step to read the sales order and email, The sales order details to the approver .

 

Conclusion

The SAP Extensibility Wizard empowers organizations running SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to extend their systems safely and efficiently, without compromising the clean core. By leveraging guided tools for custom fields, logic, and side‑by‑side processes, businesses can innovate while staying upgrade‑ready.

Extensibility in SAP is not just about adding features—it’s about doing so in a way that keeps your system future‑proof. With the wizard, you can confidently build the extensions your business needs today, while ensuring smooth upgrades tomorrow.

entry.title: SAP’s Engagement in the Eclipse IDE Working Group
entry.content:
Since 2021, SAP has been a founding member of the Eclipse IDE Working Group. It was established to ensure the long-term sustainability, integrity, and evolution of Eclipse IDE products. SAP's ABAP Development Tools (ADT) are powered by the robust Eclipse platform, allowing us to leverage its extensive capabilities and flexibility. This strategic choice enables us to innovate rapidly and deliver high-quality solutions. By actively contributing and collaborating, we help shape the platform's future, mitigate potential risks, and guarantee the availability and continuous improvement of the tools our users rely on. This is currently driven by five employees with committer status.

In this article, we would like to reflect on how we were involved with the Eclipse community on behalf of SAP and the Eclipse IDE Working Group.

Looking Back On 2025

This year was a continuation of our collaboration with the Eclipse IDE community. Beyond code contributions, SAP actively participated in various events and initiatives to support and grow the Eclipse community. Key highlights have been:

  • Eclipse shipped four releases of the Desktop IDE and we are proud that some of SAP’s contributions are listed as new and noteworthy changes in the release notes.
  • SAP hosted Eclipse Platform RCP & IDE Open Community Meetups in Germany and India, giving developers, contributors, and enthusiasts a chance to connect. We covered a wide range of topics - from community updates and progress on modernizing the Eclipse UI Toolkit to improvements on performance, security, and user experience. The agenda also included demos from Eclipse Platform committers and SAP experts about new Java features and the IBM Semuru releases, practical Eclipse IDE tips, showcases of AI features in Eclipse-based products, and sessions on improving the contributor experience. Beyond the sessions, informal discussions sparked fresh ideas, proving that open source thrives through shared effort and engagement.
    Meetup in Germany, July 2025

    Meetup in India, September 2025

  • Regular engagement in the IDE working group lead to a very productive collaboration with different working group companies and other project committers, making it possible to contribute major changes such as UI modernizations.
  • To strengthen our commitment and contribution to the Eclipse IDE ecosystem, we upgraded our membership level from "Strategic Member" to the Platinum-level of the Eclipse Foundation’s IDE Working Group in 2025.
  • Sonja Liénard (@lienards), Head of ABAP Platform and true ABAP expert, was interviewed in SAP’s podcast "The Open Source Way", sharing insights into SAP’s history of the ABAP programming language and some open source fun facts, such as even Visual Studio Code using Eclipse for the Java language support. She goes into quite some details on how SAP leverages the Eclipse IDE to provide a comprehensive and flexible development environment for its technologies. Listen to the episode at https://podcast.opensap.info/open-source-way/2025/12/01/abap-and-eclipse/. 

With these activities we aimed to give our share to collaboration and continuous investment to ensure the sustainable evolution and long-term availability of the Eclipse IDE.

Looking Ahead to 2026

Building on this strong momentum, we are looking forward to continuing our open source journey with the community to propel Eclipse forward. In 2026, we also plan to host Open Community Meetups again, providing opportunities to exchange ideas, showcase progress, and strengthen connections across the ecosystem.

Saying Thank You

A big THANKS to all colleagues, community members, and contributors who make Eclipse what it is. Every idea, contribution, and discussion helping keep the platform thriving for years to come.

Let’s keep Eclipse thriving – together!

entry.title: SAP Developer News January 8th,, 2026
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION 

CAP December 2025 release 

  • CAP December 2025 release notes https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/releases/dec25  
  • SAP Developer News episode covering Status-Transition Flows and the CAP November 2025 release https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BkCPoH7VKHg  
  • A simple exploration of status transition flows in CAP (blog post) https://qmacro.org/blog/posts/2025/12/08/a-simple-exploration-of-status-transition-flows-in-cap/  
  • Axiom AXI001 What not how https://github.com/qmacro/capref/blob/main/axioms/AXI001.md  
  • A new Hands-on SAP Dev mini-series on the core expression language in CDS (blog post) https://qmacro.org/blog/posts/2025/12/09/a-new-hands-on-sap-dev-mini-series-on-the-core-expression-language-in-cds/  
  • CDS expressions in CAP - Under the hood - Part 1 (upcoming live stream) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aiE20i5BP70  
  • Best Practice BES006 Compose with reuse in mind https://github.com/qmacro/capref/blob/main/bestpractices/BES006.md  
  • The OData "Common" Vocabulary's FieldControlType definitions https://sap.github.io/odata-vocabularies/vocabularies/Common.html#FieldControlType  

2025 set the pace, 2026 wins the race: ABAP 

  • Blog Post: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/2025-set-the-pace-2026-wins-the-race-abap-ai-with-joule-vs-code-and-ccm/ba-p/14302433 

What’s New in SAP HANA Cloud – 2025-Q4 

  • Overview: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/what-s-new-in-sap-hana-cloud-december-2025/ba-p/14295366 
  • New Machine Learning, NLP and AI features in SAP HANA Cloud 2025 Q4: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/new-machine-learning-nlp-and-ai-features-in-sap-hana-cloud-2025-q4/ba-p/14293152 
  • SAP CodeJam on Foundations of AI applications with the SAP HANA Cloud -- ML, Vectors, Knowledge Graph: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/sap-codejam-topics/ba-p/221407#codejam1  

 Application Vulnerability Report 

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/introducing-application-vulnerability-report-for-cloudfoundry-applications/ba-p/14281684 

CHAPTER TITLES  

0:00 Intro 

0:10 CAP December 2025 release 

6:20 2025 set the pace, 2026 wins the race: ABAP 

7:22 What’s New in SAP HANA Cloud – 2025-Q4 

9:36 Application Vulnerability Report 

10:32 Devtoberfest Winner Jose Pereira 

Transcript

00;00;00;08 - 00;00;05;26

This is the SAP Developer News for January 8th, 2026.

00;00;05;29 - 00;00;09;26

Over the holiday period, the CAP team delivered a nice present for us.

00;00;10;00 - 00;00;44;20

The December 2025 release. Let's take a quick look through the highlights. But before we do, let's check out the new FEATURE STATUS BADGES that are appearing more and more - in fact, there are a few in the notes for this release.

As we all know our Greek alphabet, we can correctly surmise that while BETA features are post-experimental and still possibly subject to change ... GAMMA features, not to be confused with the type of radiation that created The Incredible Hulk, are pretty much finalised and ready to use & test thoroughly.

00;00;44;22 - 00;01;19;15

Two such features that are at GAMMA level in this release are Status-Transition Flows and Declarative Constraints.

We covered Status-Transition Flows in a previous news item for the previous release, and there's an overview in this blog post we have for you.

So let's instead take a brief look at the super exciting Declarative Constraints. There are new possibilities with the @assert annotation to define complex constraints, in your CDS model.

00;01;19;18 - 00;01;50;01

That's right, declaratively. We should always strive to PUSH outwards, pushing logic up into the declarative model, and queries & data manipulation down to the database, which goes hand in hand with CAP’s axiom WHAT NOT HOW.

The conditions are expressed in another domain specific language in CDS - CXL - CDS Expression Language.

00;01;50;01 - 00;02;19;07

And, almost as if we'd planned it, there’s a new Hands-on SAP Dev live stream series on CXL starting Friday next week, with me and one of the fab members of the CAP compiler team, Patrice. Don't miss it!

CAP’s support for hierarchical tree views has just become a lot more streamlined, with a new @hierarchy annotation replacing a ton of OData specific ones.

00;02;19;08 - 00;02;46;26

Now that's what I call simple reuse, and cap is an incredibly important idea and a best practice to add in that context. There's a new command CDS export, with which you can create API client packages from your CDS service. Definitions. The output is a full featured cap reuse package that can be published to and pulled from npm registries.

00;02;46;29 - 00;03;15;23

Now that’s what I call simple.

Reuse in CAP is an incredibly important idea, and best practice too, and in that context, there’s a new command cds export with which you can create API client packages from your CDS service definitions; the output is a full-featured CAP reuse package that can be published to and pulled from NPM registries. If exporting via EDMX is the lossy MP3 audio format, then cds export gives us a lossless transfer, more like FLAC.

By the way, if you're interested to learn more about reuse in CAP, with NPM packages, we've a new blog post series Modules, modularity & reuse in CDS models which you might be interested in, with parts 1 and 2 already published.

00;03;15;26 - 00;03;45;29

In the CDS Language & Compiler topic, there's an enhancement that will appeal to those of you who develop and serve multiple frontends from the app/ directory. Now CDS file content from all ./app/* subdirectories will be fetched and loaded (say, with cds build or cds serve) instead of just from the app/ directory itself. This means we don't have to use an index.cds file in the app/ directory to import what we need.

00;03;46;01 - 00;04;19;26

Staying in the CDS Language & Compiler area, there's another enhancement that allows us to use symbols not only for enums defined in CDS, but now also for enums in OData annotations. The example in the release notes in Capire is great, because not only does it illustrate the enhancement, but it also causes the curious amongst us to use our brains and go to find out where the relation between 1 and Readonly, and between 7 and Mandatory, is defined.

00;04;19;26 - 00;04;51;01

And of course it's in OData's “Common” vocabulary, contributed by SAP.

In the Node.js runtime topic, it is now possible to make direct CRUD requests on draft-enabled entities; for example, instead of the EDIT-PATCH-SAVE sequence, you can now just send a direct UPDATE request.

Also, event queue processing has been consolidated into a default queue ... and custom queues, and there has been a change to the db-related guarantee, from ‘at least once’ to ‘exactly once’.

00;04;51;01 - 00;05;23;03

Related guarantee from at least once to exactly once. Finally, in the NodeJS runtime topic, there has been some cleanup of the model reflection APIs. Some function uses have been deprecated and the text entities are now available in a text property of the corresponding localized entity. Moving to the Java runtime topic, there are some important changes related to deep updates for draft enabled entities.

00;05;23;06 - 00;05;52;09

Finally in the Node.js runtime topic, there has been some clean up of the model reflection APIs. Some function usages have been deprecated, and the .texts entities are now available in a texts property of the corresponding localised entity.

In the Java runtime, there are some important changes related to deep updates for draft-enabled entities.

00;05;52;11 - 00;06;23;25

Now, as well as support for active entities, there’s also deep update support for inactive entities too, as long as the keys are included.

Additionally, there’s a new as(String alias) method on the CqnValue interface; with that, it’s possible to create aliased values directly, making it easier to build queries with aliased values.

In the Tools topic, there’s a Kyma convenience now for the cds up command; if your project contains Helm deployment resources, and no mta.yaml file, then you don't need to specify the --to k8s option explicitly any more.

And last but not least, in the Plugins topic, the Node.js plugin @Pa_Vi/cds-rfc now supports multitenancy.

There are links to everything I've mentioned, and more ... in the description.

00;06;23;27 - 00;06;45;04

In this blog post, Simona reflects on how 2025 laid the groundwork for AI powered ABAP development and gives us a clear line of sight into where things are going in 2026. One big highlight from the blogpost is that Joule for developers will remain free for another year, giving us more time to explore AI assisted coding in real projects.

00;06;45;07 - 00;07;15;00

The blog post walks us through the evolution of AI last year and what's to come. Smart tooling with the AI workflow, deeper integration with VS code, and a renowned focus on custom code migration using AI, all designed to help developers be more productive and modernize their code bases. And that's really the strategy going into 2020. Shipping early, scaling smart, and keeping the ABAP core clean in 2025 is about unlocking what I can do.

00;07;15;07 - 00;07;22;12

Then 2026 will make ABAP AI the core of AI about development.

00;07;22;15 - 00;07;54;05

The Q4 2025 release of SAP HANA Cloud brings many updates designed to streamline your workflow and unlock new possibilities. Let’s dive into what’s new.

Let's start with SAP HANA Cloud Central, which now supports background processing for long-running SQL statements that continue even after closing your browser. A new consolidated view of HDI schemas and containers provides quick access to HDI artifacts and their metadata.

00;07;54;08 - 00;08;36;26

SAP Business Data Cloud integration is streamlined—data products are automatically exposed as virtual tables, reducing manual setup and accelerating data reuse.

Calculation view modeling adds column lineage via SQL table functions, dynamic input parameters for MDS cube conversions, and runtime-evaluated restricted columns. Joins are enhanced with support for left outer joins in star joins.

This release introduces the ability to transform property graphs into semantic knowledge graphs without requiring redesign of data models, enabling flexible switching between paradigms on the same data for your use cases.

00;08;36;28 - 00;09;10;09

For developers and data scientists, AI and machine learning capabilities have undergone significant enhancements. The new Unified Time Series procedure provides a consistent interface across algorithms, simplifying development. Text embedding models have been improved to reduce output dimensions without sacrificing accuracy, and the new cross and convert model. In an NLP service ensures more precise search results. Ranking with the Hana Experiment Monitor user interface.

00;09;10;11 - 00;09;34;05

The final SAP CodeJam of 2025 took place in Istanbul, with a focus on AI in SAP HANA Cloud. The next one will happen in February in Berlin, and rest assured, it will be updated with some of these cool new enhancements. You can request this CodeJam topic in your location also!

00;09;34;08 - 00;09;38;22

Also see all the.

00;09;38;25 - 00;10;08;14

Why not start the year off right? With the cleanest, safest applications you can. That's what the new application vulnerability report service for on SAP. BTP focuses on Cloud Foundry applications. Is exactly for this new service. Now in beta will allow you to scan your running applications on SAP, BTP on a regular basis and look for critical vulnerabilities. Don't wait until something happens to one of your applications to find out that there's a vulnerability in it.

00;10;08;16 - 00;10;31;19

Be proactive and find out in advance. So I encourage you to check out this brand new service. I'll put the link to the blog post that describes it, as well as goes through the setup process. It's very easy to use and you can start getting your reports today, so I would encourage you to do so.

00;10;31;22 - 00;11;00;19

So my bedroom. So I hear that there were the two of us and have got a special leftovers right, to work with the community that long. You know what you want, right? Nope. There was kind of a learning journey that you did. Yeah. Our bosses always incentive us to look forward. And WhatsApp has to offer. Even if we don't do work on it.

00;11;00;21 - 00;11;22;13

It's not right. But if you know it, and that exists once the opportunity arrives, you already know it, then you can hit the ground running. Yeah. That's fantastic. So basically, as part of this learning journey, studying your cell phone, it's a party that you took part in. Definitely. Yeah. So what was your favorite aspect of that for week?

00;11;22;15 - 00;11;52;13

Yeah. I think, seeing the videos, making the quizzes, I was amazed with Joe. Yeah. Cool. Yeah. That's really cool. So did you manage to attend any of the videos live, or are you watching? Sort of, recording? Nope. I had done both. That's dedication. That's real dedication. The journey was only, to learn. We have, a few months just learning and doing things.

00;11;52;15 - 00;12;18;14

Yeah. Yeah. But in terms of, you know, what? What motivates you to keep doing. What is it? What's the secret? I think I'm a person that really wants to know more every time and keep searching and work for we can do. And, how can we automate our work? The aims, I think the I think it was all about that shit.

00;12;18;15 - 00;12;31;06

Oh, yeah. Yeah. How can we make our develop life better? Easier? And also curiosity driving. We got to learn. That's why I think that's the best definition of what developed. Yeah. Pedro, thanks very much. Thank you.

entry.title: Streamlining Production Deployment with RAP: Managing JIRA Deployment Efficiently
entry.content:
Hello Community,

Introduction:

Managing production deployments can be challenging when multiple teams and tools are involved. Our IT team relied on Excel sheets to track approved JIRA items for weekly bug fixes and monthly enhancements. This manual process was time-consuming and error-prone.

To simplify this, I developed an application using SAP RAP that automates the collection and management of JIRA items for deployment.

Challenges:

  • Tracking weekly and monthly changes across multiple teams
  • Manual Excel-based communication for access requests, JIRA closure, and stakeholder notifications
  • Difficulty in auditing past deployments

Solution Overview:

I built an application leveraging RAP draft functionality to manage deployment data efficiently.

Key Features:

  • Centralized collection of JIRA items
  • Support for attachments
  • Change History
  • Draft-enabled functionality for smooth user experience
  • Integration with number ranges for unique identifiers

Technical Objects Used:

  1. Domain
  2. Data Element
  3. Database Tables
    • Main Object (Collection of JIRA Items)
    • Sub Object (Attachments)
  4. Message Class
  5. Data Definition (CDS View)
  6. Service Definition & Service Binding (OData V4)
  7. Behavior Definition & Implementation
  8. Number Range

Benefits:

  • Eliminates manual Excel tracking
  • Provides real-time visibility of deployment items
  • Improves collaboration between IT and business stakeholders

Next Steps:

I plan to enhance the application with:

  • Workflow integration for approvals
  • Email notifications for stakeholders
  • Analytics dashboard for deployment history
⚠️Important Disclaimer
The content shared in this blog represents a conceptual idea and a demonstration only. It has not been implemented in a live environment and should not be considered as a production-ready solution. Any steps or configurations mentioned are for illustrative purposes only. Please validate and test thoroughly before applying in your SAP landscape.
 
Note
All code snippets and logic referenced in this blog will be published in the coming days on a dedicated GitHub repository. Once available, I will update this post with the repository link for your reference.

Database Table 

1. ZRELEASE_ITEMS - For main object

@EndUserText.label : 'Release Items'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #EXTENSIBLE_ANY
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED
define table zrelease_items {

  key client  : abap.clnt not null;
  key id      : abap.char(10) not null;
  key defect  : zdefectid not null;
  description : abap.char(300);
  projecttype : zproject_typ;
  region      : zregio;
  releasetype : zreltype;
  changetype  : zchange_typ;
  releasedate : abap.dats;
  ffidused    : abap.char(50);
  jiraurl     : abap.char(500);
  createdon   : abap.dats;
  createdby   : abp_creation_user;
  isdeployed  : abap_boolean;

}

2. ZRELATTACH - For attachment

@EndUserText.label : 'Release Items Attachments'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED
define table zrelattach {

  key client  : abap.clnt not null;
  key zrelid  : abap.char(10) not null;
  key zdefect : zdefectid not null;
  key zid     : uuid not null;
  attachment  : zrawstr;
  mimetype    : abap.char(128);
  filename    : abap.char(255);
  crtby       : abp_creation_user;
  crton       : abp_creation_date;

}

ZREL_CHANGELOG - For Change History

@EndUserText.label : 'release item scenario: log changes to release entity'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED
define table zrel_changelog {

  key client         : abap.clnt not null;
  key change_id      : abap.raw(16) not null;
  id                 : abap.char(10) not null;
  changing_operation : abap.char(10);
  changed_field_name : abap.char(32);
  changed_from       : abap.char(255);
  changed_value      : abap.char(255);
  created_at         : timestampl;
  changed_by         : abp_creation_user;

}

 

CDS - Basic View(Header)

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [ #NONE ]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Basic View For Release Items'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
@ObjectModel.usageType: { serviceQuality: #X, sizeCategory: #S, dataClass: #MIXED }

define root view entity ZI_RELEASE
  as select from zrelease_items
  composition [0..*] of ZI_ATTACH     as _Attach
  association [1] to ZI_PROJECT_FV as _ProjectText on  $projection.Projecttype = _ProjectText.ProjType
  association [1] to ZI_REGION_FV  as _RegionText  on  $projection.Region = _RegionText.Staging
  association [1] to ZI_RELTYP_FV  as _ReleaseText on  $projection.ReleaseType = _ReleaseText.RelType
  association [1] to ZI_CHANGE_FV  as _ChangeText  on  $projection.ChangeType = _ChangeText.ChangeTyp
  association [0..*] to  ZC_CHANGELOG as _ChangeLog on $projection.Id = _ChangeLog.Id
{
  key id          as Id,
  key defect      as Defect,
      description as Description,
      @ObjectModel.text.association: '_ProjectText'
      projecttype as Projecttype,
      @ObjectModel.text.association: '_RegionText'
      region      as Region,
      @ObjectModel.text.association: '_ReleaseText'
      releasetype as ReleaseType,
      @ObjectModel.text.association: '_ChangeText'
      changetype  as ChangeType,
      releasedate as ReleaseDate,
      ffidused    as FFID,
      jiraurl     as JiraUrl,
      createdon   as CreatedOn,
      createdby   as CreatedBy,
      isdeployed  as IsDeployed,
      _Attach,
      _ProjectText,
      _RegionText,
      _ReleaseText,
      _ChangeText,
      _ChangeLog
}

 CDS - Projection(Header) 

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Consumption View For Release Item'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
@Search.searchable: true
@ObjectModel.semanticKey: [ 'Id','Defect' ]


define root view entity ZC_RELEASE provider contract transactional_query as projection on ZI_RELEASE
{
    key Id,
    @ObjectModel.sort.enabled: true
    key Defect,
    Description,
    @ObjectModel.text.element: [ 'ProjectText' ]
    Projecttype,
    _ProjectText.Description as ProjectText,
    @ObjectModel.text.element: [ 'RegionText' ]
    Region,
    _RegionText.Description as RegionText,
    @ObjectModel.text.element: [ 'ReleaseText' ]
    ReleaseType,
    _ReleaseText.Description as ReleaseText,
    @ObjectModel.text.element: [ 'ChangeText' ]
    ChangeType,
    _ChangeText.Description as ChangeText,
    ReleaseDate,
    FFID,
    JiraUrl,
    CreatedOn,
    @Semantics.user.createdBy: true
    CreatedBy,
    IsDeployed,
    _Attach : redirected to composition child ZC_ATTACH,
    _ProjectText,
    _RegionText,
    _ReleaseText,
    _ChangeText,
    _ChangeLog
}

CDS - Basic(Attachment)

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Basic View For Attachment'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
@ObjectModel.usageType:{
    serviceQuality: #X,
    sizeCategory: #S,
    dataClass: #MIXED
}
define view entity ZI_ATTACH as select from zrelattach 
association to parent ZI_RELEASE as _RELEASE on $projection.Zrelid = _RELEASE.Id and
                                                $projection.Zdefect = _RELEASE.Defect
{
    
 key zrelattach.zrelid as Zrelid,
 key zrelattach.zdefect as Zdefect,
 key zrelattach.zid as Zid,
 zrelattach.attachment as Attachment,
 zrelattach.mimetype as Mimetype,
 zrelattach.filename as Filename,
 zrelattach.crtby as Crtby,
 zrelattach.crton as Crton,
 _RELEASE
    
}

CDS - Projection(Attachment)

{

key Zdefect,
key Zid,
@Semantics.largeObject:{

Service Definition

@EndUserText.label: 'Service definition for release item'
define service ZSD_RELEASE {
  expose ZC_RELEASE;
  expose ZC_ATTACH;
  expose ZC_CHANGELOG;
}

Service Binding

Number Range

Behavior Definition

managed implementation in class zbp_i_release unique;
strict ( 2 );
with draft;
define behavior for ZI_RELEASE //alias <alias_name>
persistent table zrelease_items
draft table ZDRAFT_RELEASE
lock master
authorization master ( instance,global )
late numbering
etag master CreatedOn
{
  create ( authorization : global );
  update(features : instance);
  delete(features : instance);
  field ( readonly ) Id;
  field ( readonly : update) Defect;
  field ( mandatory : create ) Defect; // ChangeType,Description,Region,Projecttype,ffid,ReleaseDate,ReleaseType;
  field ( features : instance )  ChangeType,Description,Region,Projecttype,ffid,ReleaseDate,ReleaseType,IsDeployed;
  association _Attach {create;with draft;}

  action ( features : instance ) MrkDep;

  draft action Edit;
  draft action Resume;
  draft action Activate optimized;
  draft action Discard;
  draft determine action Prepare
  {
    validation ValidateDD;
  }

  validation ValidateDD on save {create;update;}

 side effects
 {
   field Defect affects field JiraUrl;
 }

 determination BuildURL on modify { field Defect; }

  mapping for zrelease_items {

   Id = id;
   Defect = defect;
   ffid = ffidused;
   ChangeType = changetype;
   Description = description;
   JiraUrl = jiraurl;
   Projecttype = projecttype;
   Region = region;
   ReleaseDate = releasedate;
   ReleaseType = releasetype;
   CreatedOn = createdon;
   CreatedBy = createdby;
   IsDeployed = isdeployed;
}


}

define behavior for ZI_ATTACH
implementation in class zimpl_attach unique
persistent table zrelattach
draft table zdraft_attach
lock dependent by _RELEASE
authorization dependent by _RELEASE
late numbering
etag dependent by _RELEASE
{
    update;
    delete;
   field ( readonly:update ) Zdefect,Zrelid,Zid;
    association _RELEASE {with draft;}
        mapping for zrelattach{
    Zrelid = zrelid;
    Zdefect = zdefect;
    Zid = zid;
    Filename = filename;
    Attachment = attachment;
    Crtby = crtby;
    Crton = crton;
    Mimetype = mimetype;

    }
}

Behavior Implementation

1. Number Assignment

    LOOP AT mapped-zi_release REFERENCE INTO DATA(map).

      IF map->Id IS NOT INITIAL.
        CONTINUE.
      ENDIF.

      TRY.
          cl_numberrange_runtime=>number_get( EXPORTING object      = 'ZRELEASEID'
                                                        nr_range_nr = '01'
                                              IMPORTING number      = DATA(lv_id) ).

        CATCH cx_nr_object_not_found.
        CATCH cx_number_ranges.

      ENDTRY.

      map->Id     = |{ lv_id ALPHA = OUT }|.
      map->Defect = map->%tmp-Defect.

    ENDLOOP.

2. URL generation,

    READ ENTITIES OF zi_release IN LOCAL MODE
         ENTITY zi_release ALL FIELDS WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
         RESULT DATA(lt_results).

    LOOP AT lt_results ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_result>).

      IF <ls_result>-JiraUrl IS INITIAL.
        <ls_result>-JiraUrl = |https://Jira.company.com/browse/{ <ls_result>-Defect }|.
      ENDIF.

      IF <ls_result>-CreatedOn IS INITIAL.
        <ls_result>-CreatedOn = cl_abap_context_info=>get_system_date( ).
      ENDIF.

      IF <ls_result>-CreatedBy IS INITIAL.
        TRY.
            <ls_result>-CreatedBy = cl_abap_context_info=>get_user_technical_name(  ).
          CATCH cx_abap_context_info_error.
        ENDTRY.
      ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF zi_release IN LOCAL MODE
           ENTITY zi_release
           UPDATE FIELDS ( JiraUrl CreatedOn CreatedBy )
           WITH CORRESPONDING #( lt_results ).
  ENDMETHOD.

 

assignment of created on/created by

 

Video Snip

entry.title: “Dynamic Table Transposition in ABAP Using RTTS: A Utility-Based Approach”
entry.content:
Introduction:-

In complex SAP data processing scenarios, there is often a need to transpose data between row-oriented and column-oriented structures. This blog introduces a generic utility in ABAP that performs dynamic table transposition using RTTS (Run-Time Type Services). 

While ABAP doesn't provide a native statement like PIVOT, we can use RTTS (RunTime Type Services) to achieve a clean, reusable utility that handles dynamic transposition. 

This utility provides a way to transpose data between rows → columns and columns → rows, based on flexible input. It leverages RTTS (Run-Time Type Services) to create dynamic internal tables at runtime. 

Objective:-

Create a flexible, reusable ABAP utility that can: 

  • Convert row-based input into a column-based dynamic internal table. 
  • Convert it back from column to row format. 
  • Use RTTS to handle any number of fields without hardcoding. 

Use Case:-

Imagine this internal table:-

ROW 

FIELD 

VALUE 

1 

MATNR 

1001 

1 

MAKTX 

Material A 

1 

AMOUNT 

200.50 

2 

MATNR 

2002 

2 

MAKTX 

Material B 

2 

AMOUNT 

350.00 

We want to transpose this into:-

ROW 

MATNR 

MAKTX 

AMOUNT 

1 

1001 

Material A 

200.50 

2 

2002 

Material B 

350.00 

Code implementation--

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report ZBRT_RP_RTT_SCN
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
REPORT zbrt_rp_rtt_scn.

*Purpose  : Utility to transpose Internal Table -
*            Row to Column and Column to Row

INTERFACE zif_transposer.
  TYPES:
    BEGIN OF ty_rows,
      row    TYPE i,
      field  TYPE char30,
      dename TYPE char30,
      value  TYPE string,
    END   OF ty_rows.
  TYPES: tt_rows TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_rows.

  TYPES:
    BEGIN OF ty_fields,
      field  TYPE char30,
      dename TYPE char30,
    END   OF ty_fields.
  TYPES: tt_fields TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_fields.

  METHODS:
    transpose
      RETURNING VALUE(ro_data) TYPE REF TO data.

  DATA: t_fields     TYPE tt_fields,
        t_data       TYPE tt_rows,
        o_dyna_table TYPE REF TO data,
        o_dyna_wa    TYPE REF TO data.

  METHODS: create_dynamic_table,
    collect_fields.

ENDINTERFACE.                    "zif_transposer

CLASS zcl_transpose_to_columns DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES: zif_transposer.
    METHODS:
      constructor
        IMPORTING
          it_data   TYPE zif_transposer=>tt_rows
          it_fields TYPE zif_transposer=>tt_fields OPTIONAL.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    ALIASES: t_data   FOR zif_transposer~t_data,
             t_fields FOR zif_transposer~t_fields,
             o_dyna_table FOR zif_transposer~o_dyna_table,
             o_dyna_wa    FOR zif_transposer~o_dyna_wa.


ENDCLASS.                    "zcl_transpose_to_columns DEFINITION

CLASS zcl_transpose_to_rows DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES: zif_transposer.
    METHODS:
      constructor
        IMPORTING
          it_data   TYPE ANY TABLE
          it_fields TYPE zif_transposer=>tt_fields OPTIONAL.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    ALIASES: t_data       FOR zif_transposer~t_data,
             t_fields     FOR zif_transposer~t_fields,
             o_dyna_table FOR zif_transposer~o_dyna_table,
             o_dyna_wa    FOR zif_transposer~o_dyna_wa.

ENDCLASS.                    "zcl_transpose_to_rows DEFINITION

CLASS zcl_transpose_to_columns IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD constructor.
    t_data   = it_data.
    t_fields = it_fields.

  ENDMETHOD.                    "constructor
  METHOD zif_transposer~transpose.
    FIELD-SYMBOLS:
      <f_tab>   TYPE STANDARD TABLE,
      <f_wa>    TYPE any,
      <f_field> TYPE any.
    DATA: ls_data LIKE LINE OF me->t_data.

*   create dynamic table
    me->zif_transposer~collect_fields( ).
    me->zif_transposer~create_dynamic_table( ).

*   Move data to Dynamica table
    ASSIGN o_dyna_table->*  TO <f_tab>.
    ASSIGN o_dyna_wa->*     TO <f_wa>.

    LOOP AT me->t_data INTO ls_data.
      ASSIGN COMPONENT ls_data-field OF STRUCTURE <f_wa> TO <f_field>.
      IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
        <f_field> = ls_data-value.
      ENDIF.
      AT END OF row.
        APPEND <f_wa> TO <f_tab>.
        CLEAR  <f_wa>.
      ENDAT.
    ENDLOOP.
*   send data back
    ro_data = o_dyna_table.

  ENDMETHOD.                    "zif_transposer~transpose

  METHOD zif_transposer~create_dynamic_table.

    DATA: lo_struct   TYPE REF TO cl_abap_structdescr,
          lo_element  TYPE REF TO cl_abap_elemdescr,
          lo_new_type TYPE REF TO cl_abap_structdescr,
          lo_new_tab  TYPE REF TO cl_abap_tabledescr,
          lt_comp     TYPE cl_abap_structdescr=>component_table,
          lt_tot_comp TYPE cl_abap_structdescr=>component_table,
          la_comp     LIKE LINE OF lt_comp.

    DATA: ls_field LIKE LINE OF me->t_fields,
          lv_name  TYPE string.

    LOOP AT me->t_fields INTO ls_field.
      IF ls_field-dename IS INITIAL.
        lv_name = ls_field-field.
      ELSE.
        lv_name = ls_field-dename.
      ENDIF.

*   Element Description
      lo_element ?= cl_abap_elemdescr=>describe_by_name( lv_name ).

*   Components
      la_comp-name = ls_field-field.
      la_comp-type = lo_element.
      APPEND la_comp TO lt_tot_comp.
      CLEAR: la_comp.
    ENDLOOP.

*   Create a New Type
    lo_new_type = cl_abap_structdescr=>create( lt_tot_comp ).

*   New Table type
    lo_new_tab = cl_abap_tabledescr=>create(
                    p_line_type  = lo_new_type
                    p_table_kind = cl_abap_tabledescr=>tablekind_std
                    p_unique     = abap_false ).

*   data to handle the new table type
    CREATE DATA : o_dyna_table TYPE HANDLE lo_new_tab,
                  o_dyna_wa    TYPE HANDLE lo_new_type.

  ENDMETHOD.                    "create_dynamic_table
  METHOD zif_transposer~collect_fields.

    CHECK t_fields IS INITIAL.

    DATA: lv_first_field TYPE char30,
          ls_data        LIKE LINE OF me->t_data,
          ls_field       LIKE LINE OF me->t_fields.

    LOOP AT me->t_data INTO ls_data.
      IF lv_first_field = ls_data-field.
        EXIT.
      ENDIF.
      IF lv_first_field IS INITIAL.
        lv_first_field = ls_data-field.
      ENDIF.
      MOVE-CORRESPONDING ls_data TO ls_field.
      APPEND ls_field TO me->t_fields.
    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.                    "collect_Fields
ENDCLASS.                    "zcl_transpose_to_columns IMPLEMENTATION

*
CLASS zcl_transpose_to_rows IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD constructor.
    GET REFERENCE OF it_data INTO o_dyna_table.
    t_fields = it_fields.
  ENDMETHOD.                    "constructor

  METHOD zif_transposer~transpose.

    FIELD-SYMBOLS:
      <f_tab>   TYPE STANDARD TABLE,
      <f_wa>    TYPE any,
      <f_field> TYPE any.

    DATA: lt_rows   TYPE zif_transposer=>tt_rows,
          ls_rows   LIKE LINE OF lt_rows,
          lv_row    TYPE i,
          ls_fields LIKE LINE OF t_fields.

    me->zif_transposer~collect_fields( ).
    me->zif_transposer~create_dynamic_table( ).

    ASSIGN o_dyna_table->* TO <f_tab>.

    LOOP AT <f_tab> ASSIGNING <f_wa>.
      lv_row = sy-tabix.

      LOOP AT t_fields INTO ls_fields.

        ASSIGN COMPONENT ls_fields-field OF STRUCTURE <f_wa> TO <f_field>.
        IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
          ls_rows-row   = lv_row.
          ls_rows-field = ls_fields-field.
          ls_rows-dename = ls_fields-dename.
          ls_rows-value  = <f_field>.
          APPEND ls_rows TO lt_rows.
        ENDIF.

      ENDLOOP.

    ENDLOOP.

    me->t_data = lt_rows.
    GET REFERENCE OF me->t_data INTO ro_data.

  ENDMETHOD.                    "zif_transposer~transpose

  METHOD zif_transposer~create_dynamic_table.

*   Setting up the o_dyna_wa is unnecessary, just sake of completeness
*   of this method
    FIELD-SYMBOLS:
      <f_tab> TYPE STANDARD TABLE,
      <f_wa>  TYPE any.

    ASSIGN o_dyna_table->* TO <f_tab>.
    READ TABLE <f_tab> ASSIGNING <f_wa> INDEX 1.
    IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
      GET REFERENCE OF <f_wa> INTO o_dyna_wa.
    ENDIF.

  ENDMETHOD.                    "zif_transposer~create_dynamic_table

  METHOD zif_transposer~collect_fields.

    DATA: lo_tab    TYPE REF TO cl_abap_tabledescr,
          lo_struc  TYPE REF TO cl_abap_structdescr,
          lt_comp   TYPE cl_abap_structdescr=>component_table,
          ls_comp   LIKE LINE OF lt_comp,
          ls_fields LIKE LINE OF t_fields.

    IF t_fields IS INITIAL.
      lo_tab ?= cl_abap_tabledescr=>describe_by_data_ref( o_dyna_table ).
      lo_struc ?= lo_tab->get_table_line_type( ).
      lt_comp = lo_struc->get_components( ).

      LOOP AT lt_comp INTO ls_comp.
        ls_fields-field = ls_comp-name.
        ls_fields-dename = ls_comp-type->get_relative_name( ).
        APPEND ls_fields TO t_fields.
      ENDLOOP.

    ENDIF.

  ENDMETHOD.                    "zif_transposer~collect_fields
ENDCLASS.                    "zcl_transpose_to_rows IMPLEMENTATION


*Demo Program
*Here is the simple demo program to test the utility class

START-OF-SELECTION.
* test data
  DATA: lt_rows TYPE zif_transposer=>tt_rows,
        ls_rows LIKE LINE OF lt_rows.

  lt_rows = VALUE #(
    ( row = 1 field = 'MATNR' value = '1-234-123' )
    ( row = 1 field = 'MAKTX' value = 'Test Material 1' )
    ( row = 1 field = 'AMOUNT' dename = 'DMBTR' value = '123.45' )
    ( row = 2 field = 'MATNR' value = '9-123-123' )
    ( row = 2 field = 'MAKTX' value = 'Assembly 123' )
  ).

* Transpose the data to columns
  DATA: lo_col_transposer TYPE REF TO zif_transposer,
        lo_col_data       TYPE REF TO data.

  FIELD-SYMBOLS:<f_output> TYPE STANDARD TABLE.

  CREATE OBJECT lo_col_transposer
    TYPE
    zcl_transpose_to_columns
    EXPORTING
      it_data = lt_rows.
  lo_col_data = lo_col_transposer->transpose( ).
  ASSIGN lo_col_data->* TO <f_output>.

  DATA: o_salv TYPE REF TO cl_salv_table.
  TRY.
      CALL METHOD cl_salv_table=>factory
        IMPORTING
          r_salv_table = o_salv
        CHANGING
          t_table      = <f_output>.
      o_salv->display( ).
    CATCH cx_salv_msg .
  ENDTRY.


* Transpose to rows
  DATA: lo_row_transposer TYPE REF TO zif_transposer,
        lo_row_data       TYPE REF TO data.

  FIELD-SYMBOLS: <lfs_row_tab> TYPE zif_transposer=>tt_rows.

  CREATE OBJECT lo_row_transposer
    TYPE
    zcl_transpose_to_rows
    EXPORTING
      it_data = <f_output>.
  lo_row_data = lo_row_transposer->transpose( ).
  ASSIGN lo_row_data->* TO <lfs_row_tab>.

  DATA: o_salv2 TYPE REF TO cl_salv_table.
  TRY.
      CALL METHOD cl_salv_table=>factory
        IMPORTING
          r_salv_table = o_salv2
        CHANGING
          t_table      = <lfs_row_tab>.
      o_salv2->display( ).
    CATCH cx_salv_msg .
  ENDTRY.

Key Components-- 

Interface ZIF_TRANSPOSER 

Defines shared structure and behavior:-

Types:-

  • TY_ROWS: For input/output row-style data. 
  • TY_FIELDS: Metadata about field names and domain names. 

Attributes:-

  • T_DATA: Holds row-wise data. 
  • T_FIELDS: Holds metadata. 
  • O_DYNA_TABLE, O_DYNA_WA: Dynamic table and work area.

Methods:-

  • TRANSPOSE: Main method to perform transformation. 
  • CREATE_DYNAMIC_TABLE: Creates dynamic internal table from metadata. 
  • COLLECT_FIELDS: Collects field metadata.

Class ZCL_TRANSPOSE_TO_COLUMNS

Purpose:-

Transpose row-oriented data into a column-based dynamic internal table. 

Implements row → column transposition.

METHOD zif_transposer~transpose.

    FIELD-SYMBOLS:
      <f_tab>   TYPE STANDARD TABLE,
      <f_wa>    TYPE any,
      <f_field> TYPE any.
    DATA: ls_data LIKE LINE OF me->t_data.

*   create dynamic table
    me->zif_transposer~collect_fields( ).
    me->zif_transposer~create_dynamic_table( ).

*   Move data to Dynamica table
    ASSIGN o_dyna_table->*  TO <f_tab>.
    ASSIGN o_dyna_wa->*     TO <f_wa>.

    LOOP AT me->t_data INTO ls_data.
      ASSIGN COMPONENT ls_data-field OF STRUCTURE <f_wa> TO <f_field>.
      IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
        <f_field> = ls_data-value.
      ENDIF.
      AT END OF row.
        APPEND <f_wa> TO <f_tab>.
        CLEAR  <f_wa>.
      ENDAT.
    ENDLOOP.
*   send data back
    ro_data = o_dyna_table.
  ENDMETHOD.                    "zif_transposer~transpose

TRANSPOSE:-

Uses collect_fields and create_dynamic_table to prepare output table structure. 

Loops over t_data:-

  • Groups by ROW number. 
  • Assigns each value to its respective field in the dynamic work area. 
  • Appends when end of the row is reached.

Class ZCL_TRANSPOSE_TO_ROWS 

Purpose:-

Convert dynamic internal tables back into row-wise data. 

Implements column → row transposition.

CLASS zcl_transpose_to_rows IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD constructor.
    GET REFERENCE OF it_data INTO o_dyna_table.
    t_fields = it_fields.
  ENDMETHOD.                    "constructor

  METHOD zif_transposer~transpose.

    FIELD-SYMBOLS:
      <f_tab>   TYPE STANDARD TABLE,
      <f_wa>    TYPE any,
      <f_field> TYPE any.
    DATA: lt_rows   TYPE zif_transposer=>tt_rows,
          ls_rows   LIKE LINE OF lt_rows,
          lv_row    TYPE i,
          ls_fields LIKE LINE OF t_fields.

    me->zif_transposer~collect_fields( ).
    me->zif_transposer~create_dynamic_table( ).

    ASSIGN o_dyna_table->* TO <f_tab>.
    LOOP AT <f_tab> ASSIGNING <f_wa>.
      lv_row = sy-tabix.

      LOOP AT t_fields INTO ls_fields.
        ASSIGN COMPONENT ls_fields-field OF STRUCTURE <f_wa> TO <f_field>.
        IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
          ls_rows-row   = lv_row.
          ls_rows-field = ls_fields-field.
          ls_rows-dename = ls_fields-dename.
          ls_rows-value  = <f_field>.
          APPEND ls_rows TO lt_rows.
        ENDIF.
      ENDLOOP.
    ENDLOOP.
    me->t_data = lt_rows.
    GET REFERENCE OF me->t_data INTO ro_data.

  ENDMETHOD.                    "zif_transposer~transpose

TRANSPOSE:-

Loops through dynamic table rows (<f_tab>). 

For each field in each row:-

  • Reads value via field-symbol.
  • Stores as row-wise t_data entries (tt_rows).

Helper Methods 

COLLECT_FIELDS

If no metadata was provided, derive field names and types: 

  • From row data (ZCL_TRANSPOSE_TO_COLUMNS).
  • Or from table structure via RTTS (ZCL_TRANSPOSE_TO_ROWS).

CREATE_DYNAMIC_TABLE:-

  • Uses cl_abap_structdescr and cl_abap_tabledescr to create table based on metadata. 
  • Stores the table and line reference in o_dyna_table, o_dyna_wa.

Sample Test Program:-

Input Data:-

Transpose to Columns:-

Transpose Back to Rows:-

Display via SALV:-

Let's execute it and see the result:-

After Rows to Columns.

After Columns to Rows, which is same as what we have started with the test data.

What Makes It Powerful:-

  • Generic → No hardcoded structure. 
  • Reusable → Accepts any row-format or table-format. 
  • Dynamic → Fully uses RTTS (Run-Time Type Services). 
  • Extensible → You can plug it into other data manipulation utilities or UI tools.

Conclusion:-

Dynamic data transposition is a common need in flexible reporting and interface design. By leveraging RTTS, this utility provides a powerful, generic, and elegant solution to this challenge. It abstracts away the technical complexity, offering a clean interface to both functional and technical consultants.

Thanks__

entry.title: SAP BTP ABAP Environment Customer & Partner Roundtable – Update for 2026
entry.content:
We would like to share an update to the SAP BTP ABAP Environment Customer & Partner Roundtable as we kick off the 2026 session series.

What’s new in 2026?

The roundtable is a monthly series taking place on the first Tuesday of every month 10:00 - 11:00 CET.
